design_philosophy.core_loop: Climb vertically through monster-filled chambers, collect hearts from defeated enemies, use hearts as currency for upgrades and healing in shops/temples, reach fortress and defeat boss to claim sacred treasure
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Resource scarcity (hearts as currency and health) vs. progression pressure; weapon upgrades require sustained health, creating risk/reward in combat encounters
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Pit, a winged angel, ascending through three mythological worlds (Underworld, Overworld, Skyworld) to retrieve sacred treasures and rescue Angel Land from Medusa's darkness
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Hybrid genre design mixing vertical platforming with dungeon exploration, RPG-lite progression (stat increases via chambers), and resource management via heart economy; final stage abandons platforming entirely for scrolling shooter mechanics, demanding genre versatility

difficulty.lives_system: Continues system with password-based progression (NES version); FDS version uses save slots. Taking damage while at 1 health can result in continuing with 0 health (exploitable mechanic)
difficulty.continues_system: Continues available; FDS ending calculation penalizes continues used (-1 point per continue), creating incentive to minimize usage
difficulty.checkpointing: Stage-based checkpointing via passwords (NES) or save files (FDS). No mid-level checkpoints; death forces restart of current stage/fortress
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Limited direct difficulty selection, but significant player agency through resource management: players choose which chambers to enter, which upgrades to purchase, how much to farm hearts. Health threshold gating on weapon upgrades creates meaningful strategic decisions

progression.upgrade_system: Three weapon tiers (Protective Crystal, Flaming Arrows, Sacred Bow) that require health thresholds to activate. Strength and Endurance stats increase via training/god chambers based on performance (enemies defeated, damage taken). Hearts increase max health when threshold collected at level end
progression.unlock_mechanism: Three sacred treasures obtained by defeating fortress bosses (Underworld, Overworld, Skyworld gatekeepers); all three required to access final Sky Temple stage. Each stage is 3 climbing levels + 1 fortress
replayability_hooks: Multiple endings based on stat distribution (Strength, Endurance, Hearts, Weapons on NES; Strength, Endurance, Continues on FDS)
Score tracking and high-score lists (FDS version) or password challenges (NES version)
Password system enables skipping to later stages with modified conditions (Nintendo Power classified password enables invincibility and stage-skip)
Variable difficulty through chamber selection and farming decisions affecting final stat values

world_building_techniques: Environmental storytelling through stage progression: climb from darkness (Underworld) through light (Overworld) to sky (Skyworld), mirroring mythological ascent
Greek mythology theming with Palutena (Goddess of Light), Medusa (Goddess of Darkness), Centurion allies
Chamber types serve narrative function: god's chambers invoke divine blessing, hot springs provide sanctuary, treasure rooms hint at angel land's riches
NES version final stage transformation from platformer to shooter represents Pit's final ascent to celestial battle

boss_design.philosophy: Fortress gatekeepers serve as stage climax encounters; Medusa as final boss represents narrative culmination. Boss design emphasizes pattern recognition and resource preparation (Centurion allies available via mallet item)
notable_bosses: Medusa (final boss, Sky Temple) - NES version shows her collapse after defeat

faction_indicators: Palutena (Goddess of Light) as ally/guide, sending Pit on quest
Medusa (Goddess of Darkness) as antagonist, conspiring to corrupt Angel Land
Centurion soldiers as divine allies summoned to aid Pit in fortress battles
danger_signals: Eggplant Wizards transform Pit, representing body horror corruption threat
Medusa's surprise attack and theft of palace treasure establishes urgent threat
Progression through Underworld (realm of monsters and darkness) signals increasing danger before final confrontation
Stone-turned humans referenced in plot establish stakes of Medusa's corruption
environmental_storytelling: Three worlds layered vertically: Underworld (monsters, darkness) → Overworld (human realm, vulnerability) → Skyworld (celestial, divine)
Fortress labyrinths with purchasable navigation items (map, torch) represent civilization vs. chaos
Hot springs, temples, and shops within dungeons suggest sacred spaces protecting against corruption
Final stage as scrolling shooter in sky temple represents transition from mortal platforming to divine aerial combat
